observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Market Committee to convene and review the provisions Section 21 of the Municipal Ordinance No. 04 Series of 2012 as to its applicability and to determine whether necessary revisions are needed to improve the market operations and to come up with an advantageous solution to both the LGU and the lessors.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the PMR of the Municipality and submit it to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance to Section 12.2 of Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Coordinate and reconcile the records with Municipal Assessors Office and Inventory and Appraisal Committee to ascertain the correctness of the Land account.
Prepare Journal Entry Vouchers to record the cost/value assigned by the Inventory and Appraisal Committee based on the market/fair value for properties found at the station.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Analyze the Loans Payable and its related interest to ensure proper recognition of the current and non-current portion; and
Prepare an adjusting entry to rectify the errors in recording the interest and principal portion of payment committed in prior years.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the accrued expenses, i.e accrued interest, are recorded in accordance with accrual basis and International Public Sector Accounting Standards, moving forward.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide adequate disclosures on Loans Payable account so that the liquidity and solvency of the Municipality could be properly assessed.
We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a special account in the General Fund for all loans availed by the municipality.

We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Update themselves on the rules and regulations pertaining to the warranty security required in the procurement of goods; and
Ensure that for future procurements, retain an amount equivalent to at least 1% of the contract price or require a special bank guarantee of the same amount from the suppliers,  to guarantee that that supplier will be liable for any manufacturing defects.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to discontinue the payment of mid-year bonus, year-end bonus, and cash gift earlier than the prescribed period. Also, strictly adhere to the prescribed guidelines on the release of the mandatory benefits for future transactions.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Update themselves on the rules and regulations pertaining to the transfer of funds to the other agencies; and
Ensure that all future disbursement vouchers relating to the transfer of funds are adequately supported with mandatory documentary requirements before processing the transaction.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Health Officer to submit the approved DTRs, accomplishment reports, and office order/ work arrangements; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims against government funds, especially the SRA and AHDP, are supported with complete documentary requirements as provided under Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree (PD) of 1445.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the Perpetual Inventory Method of accounting, by taking up first the construction materials procured, under the Construction Materials Inventory, upon purchase, in compliance with the above-mentioned rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Management:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to require the contractors to create an As-Built Plan in future completed projects, whether NGA or locally-funded, prior to issuance of Certificate of Completion; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursement vouchers relating to the final payment of infrastructure projects are adequately supported with mandatory documentary requirements before processing the payments.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure proper recording of transactions to avoid such errors so that the propriety and validity of the recorded transactions are maintained.

We recommended that Management:

a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a Schedule of Unreleased Checks at the end of the year, and immediately submit the said schedule to the Municipal Accounting Office; and

b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts every year-end for the total amount of unreleased checks, to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a) Prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JV) to adjust the properties below the capitalization threshold and its related accounts, as follows:

Account                            Debit  Credit
 
Government Equity             470,868.63
Accumulated Depreciation Accounts       629,378.75
PPE Accounts        1,100,247.38
To drop non-capitalizable PPEs from CY 2001 to 2020

Supplies and Materials Expense        95,168.00     
 Surplus (Deficit) Account       31,900.82
  Depreciation Expense       127,068.82 
  To reclassify the non-capitalizable properties acquired in CY 2020 

b) Ensure that all future acquisitions of properties below the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000 will be recorded in accordance with Section 5.4 of COA Circular 2016-006 and Chapter II Section 4.ee of GAM for LGU Volume I.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a.) Adjust the Accumulated Depreciation's misstatement accordingly to rectify the error of recognizing the residual value and present the Road Network account to its correct carrying amount.

b.) Ensure that the Municipal Accounting Office is updated on the latest provisions regarding computation of depreciation on PPE Accounts.

We recommended that Management:

a.)Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities; and

b.) Ensure the full implementation of "One-time cleansing of PPE accounts" as provided under COA Circular 2020-006.

We recommended Management to ensure the completeness, proper recognition and valuation of the Inventory of Accountable Forms by requiring the:

Municipal Accountant to-
a. Include in the Inventory account all Accountable Forms (with and without money value) as provided in Section 175 of GAM Vol. 1 to ensure completeness of the items included in the Financial Statements;

b. Record the purchases as Inventory and adhere to the provisions of Section 181 on the proper measurement of the Inventory at year-end to ensure accuracy of the amounts reported;

c. Prepare an adjusting entry to correct the balance of the affected accounts; and

Municipal Treasurer to-
d. Prepare a separate logbook for Accountable Forms held for consumption/distribution from those held for sale for proper recording and monitoring of the assets.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to: a.)Locate/obtain the documents of the motor vehicles and facilitate the processing of registrations and insurance coverage of the motor vehicles and;b.) Regularly monitor the expiration and renewal of all motor vehicles registration to avoid penalties or fines for late/non-registration.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with Sections 4 & 214 of GAM for LGUs Volume I, and prepare adjusting entry   to   take   up   the understatement of the asset and depreciation expense using the following suggested adjusting journal entries:

                       Account                 Debit             Credit
 
Buildings and Other Structures          770,060.34
Prior Years Adjustment           770,060.34
To record PPE account erroneously charged to expense

Prior Years Adjustment            6,096.31
 Accumulated Depreciation-B & OS                               6,096.31
To record accumulated depreciation of the completed projects charged to expenses

We recommended Management to:

a.) Direct the BAC Secretariat to immediately comply with the GPPB Online Portal's posting requirement for Emergency Procurement under Republic Act 11469 (Bayanihan to Heal as One Act). 

b.) Capacitate the BAC Secretariat on the laws, rules and regulations on government procurement; 

c.) Require the BAC Secretariat to promote transparency in government procurement by ensuring that the Notices of Award and Purchase Orders for future procurements are posted in accordance with the guidelines prescribed.

We recommended that Management adhere to the procurement process in the implementation of programs and activities to address the COVID-19 provided in  the Revised IRR of RA 9184, and discourage the grant cash advances to disbursing officers for the procurement of goods in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended Management to:

a.) Require the Local Finance Committee to ensure that the financial information of Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities are made available to the public, in adherence to the prescribed guidelines outlined in the Local Budget Circular No. 125;

b.) Expedite the creation of the Municipal Government's Official Website; and

c.) Require the Local Finance Committee to keep themselves updated with the latest provisions and guidelines pertaining to their functions.

We recommended that Management:

a.) Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a Special Account in the General Fund along with its complete subsidiary ledgers for the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities and future Other Special Accounts.

b.) Instruct the Local Finance Committee to keep themselves updated with the latest provisions and guidelines pertaining to their functions.

We recommended that Management direct the MDRRM Officer to: 

a.) Update themselves to the latest policies and guidelines on Donations In-Kind as provided by the relevant and competent government offices; and

b.) Ensure the on-time preparation and submission of the Report of the Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to the Office of the Civil Defense and Auditor’s Office, in compliance to Section 2(b) of COA Circular 2020-009, moving forward.

We recommended Management to direct the:

   Market Supervisor to-

a.) Update and reconcile its financial records to arrive at the corrected balances as of December 31, 2020;

b.) Furnish the Municipal Accountant with the contract of lease and the schedule of uncollected balance at year-end. 

Municipal Accountant to-

c.) Prepare an adjusting entry to record the uncollected balances of rent from stallholders at year-end;

d.) Ensure that succeeding revenues will be recorded on an accrual basis pursuant to Section 41 of GAM for LGUs.
We recommended that Management allocate budget to hire an external consultant to prepare the 5-year business plan as prescribed under Section 4.6 of the Local Budget Circular 111 dated June 10, 2016.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant the documents required for the setting up of RPT and SET Receivable pursuant to Section 20 of NGAS Vol. 1 on the next ensuing years and instruct the Municipal Account to facilitate reconciliation of the balances reflecting the balances in the duly certified list of taxpayers.
observations.managementAction: Encoding is still on process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Local Economic Enterprise –In Charge to prepare the 5-year business plan or hire an external consultant if needed as prescribed by Section 4.6 of the Local Budget Circular 111.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System adhere to the provisions of Section 3.0 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2016-01 and facilitate the conduct of gender analysis using the HGDG tool for gender-responsive PPAs through its GAD Focal Point System
We recommended that Management to initiate the processing of documents for the transfer of ownership of lands owned by the LGU pursuant to the provisions of Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: Management is currently processing the titling of the lands owned by the LGU

Ensure that the Municipal Accounting Office is updated on the latest provisions regarding computation of depreciation on PPE Accounts.

Buildings and Other Structures      		  770,060.34
Prior Years Adjustment						  770,060.34
To record PPE account erroneously charged to expense
Prior Years Adjustment				  6,096.31
Accumulated Depreciation-B & OS 					                        6,096.31
To record accumulated depreciation of the completed projects charged to expenses

Adhere to the procurement process in the implementation of programs and activities to address the COVID-19 provided in the Revised IRR of RA 9184, and discourage the grant cash advances to disbursing officers for the procurement of goods in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a Special Account in the General Fund along with its complete subsidiary ledgers for the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities and future Other Special Accounts; and
Instruct the Local Finance Committee to keep themselves updated with the latest provisions and guidelines pertaining to their functions.

Update themselves to the latest policies and guidelines on Donations In-Kind as provided by the relevant and competent government offices; and
Ensure the on-time preparation and submission of the Report of the Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to the Office of the Civil Defense and Auditor’s Office, in compliance to Section 2(b) of COA Circular 2020-009, moving forward.

Direct the Market Committee to convene and review the provisions Section 21 of the Municipal Ordinance No. 04 Series of 2012 as to its applicability and to determine whether necessary revisions are needed to improve the market operations and to come up with an advantageous solution to both the LGU and the lessors.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review and check the RPCPPE, reclassify the unserviceable properties to appropriate accounts, drop the coco coir building from its PPE schedule and transfer the accountability to the beneficiary

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the Loans Payable to current and non-current liabilities in the Financial Statements and to provide disclosures on the Notes to Financial Statements, the amortizations of the loans for the fair presentation of accounts.

We recommended that the MSWDO require the submission of the necessary documents for the assessment of the individuals or families seeking help to ensure that only those eligible will be granted, submit complete  documentation under the DSWD Memorandum Circular 2019-11 prior to the processing of the claims and refrain from processing the Disbursement Vouchers without or with incomplete supporting documents to ensure propriety and validity of the transactions in compliance to Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point to include in the submission of the GAD AR the reports/documents mentioned in Section 4.C.8.5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 to facilitate the monitoring, evaluation and substantiation of the reported accomplishments.

We recommended that Bids and Awards Committee submit a copy of purchase orders and their supporting documents, regardless of the amount, to the Auditor’s Office within the prescribed period as provided in Item 3.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-001 for the timely review of the transaction.

We recommended that the LGU formulate and pass an ordinance to aid the implementation of waste segregation and proper disposal, and strictly enforce the provisions of RA 9003 to protect its constituents from possible threats and hazards. Further, direct the garbage collectors to segregate, store and sell the plastics, cans, and other recyclables to prospective buyers in order to reduce the waste disposed on the landfill while at the same time, generating income out of the recyclable wastes.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify the actual days rendered of the Social Worker and review the computation on the grant of hazard and subsistence allowance. Also, make necessary computation and cause the refund of the overpaid amount and observe the limitation in the entitlement of the allowance pursuant to DBM-DSWD Joint Circular No. 1 Series of 2018.

We recommended that Human Resource Management Officer require those non-member Job Orders to submit their application for membership to the HDMF Office as provided under Section 1(4), Rule V of RA 9679, and to furnish their office a copy of the Membership ID Number for the remittance of their contributions. Further, deduct the ₱200.00 (₱100.00 employee and ₱100.00 employer share) HDMF contributions to their wages and remit the same to the HMDF Office.
We recommended that Management allocate budget to hire an external consultant to prepare the 5-year business plan as prescribed under Section 4.6 of the Local Budget Circular 111 dated June 10, 2016.

We recommended that the Management direct the Local Economic Enterprise –In Charge to prepare the 5-year business plan or hire an external consultant if needed as prescribed by Section 4.6 of the Local Budget Circular 111.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant the documents required for the setting up of RPT and SET Receivable pursuant to Section 20 of NGAS Vol. 1 on the next ensuing years and instruct the Municipal Account to facilitate reconciliation of the balances reflecting the balances in the duly certified list of taxpayers.

observations.reason: Encoding is still on process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the amounts of Investment Property and Property, Plant and Equipment Accounts as stated in the Financial Statements; And submit the Report of the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment not later than January 31 of each year.
observations.reason: The adjustments were reflected to the 2019 financial statements

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Accounting Office to prepare the Report in Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF on a monthly basis and direct the LDRRMO to submit before the 15th day after the end of each month as required by Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.reason: The 2019 Reports were submitted to the office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the Market Supervisor to prepare and maintain a subsidiary ledger for the market mall consisting of the necessary information of the occupants and the payments of the rental fee. Also, to enforce payment on the delinquent tenants and impose a penalty, if necessary. Moreover, direct the Local Development Council to review the rates of the rental fees on the market mall and to prepare the necessary amendment of its operation, if needed.
observations.reason: The Market Supervisor now maintains the necessary records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strengthen its planning process in identifying and anticipating the activities to be undertaken and the cost to be incurred in holding huge events to ensure that appropriations for the activities are sufficient.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System adhere to the provisions of Section 3.0 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2016-01 and facilitate the conduct of gender analysis using the HGDG tool for gender-responsive PPAs through its GAD Focal Point System.
observations.managementAction: In our case HGDG is not applicable because our activities under GAD is below 1M.  Based on the scheduled activities as of June 30, 2019  especially on women celebration, recognition program of child development centers were fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly adhere the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of the DBM and DILG in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
observations.reason: The activities in the to be funded by the 20%  for 2019 were in compliance to the JMC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management exert extra effort to fully implement its priority programs and projects that are envisioned to help in the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental outcomes; resort to contracting some of its projects to attain economy and efficiency in project implementation; the Municipal Engineer to fast-track the preparation of the program of works; the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to properly plan procurement activities to avoid delayed implementation of projects; the  Local Development Council to judiciously plan the inclusion of priority projects to be included in the 20% Development Fund to avoid revisions and realignment; and the Local Chief Executive to re-create the Project Monitoring Committee/Project Inspection Team that would assist, evaluate and monitor the extent of implementation of programs and projects in order to determine the causes of delay in the implementation.
observations.reason: The projects were implemented for 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Sections 37 of Republic Act 9003 in order to deliver to its constituents the maximum benefits of which this Act is created.
observations.reason: The Open dumpsite was closed and they are now using their new dumpsite.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the BUB Focal Person to monitor the activities to be undertaken to ensure that the project will be completed the soonest possible time since it is already delayed for more than 1 year; and instruct the Municipal Engineer to fast track the completion of the building and ensure that quality service will be delivered to its intended beneficiaries as provided by DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC JMC No. 7.
observations.reason: Project completed and transferred to beneficiary

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Development Council identify program, projects and activities that partakes the nature of investment or capital expenditure and ensure that only those that will contribute directly to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes will be funded from the 20 % development fund as required under DILG-DBM JMC 2017-1.
observations.reason: This finding is reiterated in our 2018 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF to the Auditor on the required date to facilitate the timely audit of the same and that any defect/deficiency noted could be immediately corrected and promptly communicated to management for appropriate action
observations.reason: Reports were submitted to the Office every month.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to initiate the processing of documents for the transfer of ownership of lands owned by the LGU pursuant to the provisions of Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: Management is currently processing the titling of the lands owned by the LGU
observations.reason: As of Dec. 31, 2018, two (2) lands were titled in favor of LGU Lianga

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare the Semi-Annual Financial Self-Sufficiency Review (SFSSR) Report for every Local Economic Enterprises (LEE) starting CY 2018.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Sections 37 of Republic Act 9003 - “An Act Providing for an Ecological Solid Waste Program, Creating the Necessary Institutional Mechanisms and Incentives, Declaring Certain Acts Prohibited and Providing Penalties, Appropriating Funds therefor, and Other Purposes”, to deliver to its constituents the maximum benefits of which this Act is created.
observations.reason: The open dump site was closed and they are now using the disposal facility.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/Property Custodian send demand letters to the concerned accountable officers for the production of the missing government properties and/or report explaining why they were not found during the physical count. Also, advise the accountable officer to request relief from accountability to COA.
observations.reason: The unaccounted PPEs were included in the 2019 inventory report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

1.    Prioritize for funding the acquisition of additional equipment for the collection and disposal of solid waste so that only properly segregated wastes are deposited at the dump site thereby preventing the spilling of garbage to the nearby areas as what happened in the previous year/s.

2.      Follow-up the grant of the BUB for the access road to their new dumpsite for them to expedite transfer and to lessen the disposal of non-segregated waste on their current dumpsite.

3.       Encourage the garbage collectors to segregate, store and sell the plastics, cans and other recyclables to prospective buyers in order to reduce the waste disposed on their open dump site while at the same time, generating income out of the recyclable wastes.

We recommended that the Local Development Council identify program, projects and activities that partakes the nature of investment or capital expenditure and ensure that only those that will contribute directly to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes will be funded from the 20 % development fund as required under DILG-DBM JMC 2017-1.
observations.reason: This finding is reiterated in our 2018 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the GAD Focal Point to include in the submission of the GAD AR the reports/documents mentioned in Section 4.C.8.5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 to facilitate the monitoring, evaluation and substantiation of the reported accomplishments.
observations.reason: This audit observation is reiterated on the 2017 report.

observations.recommendation: 1.5 We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant the documents required for the setting up of RPT and SET Receivable pursuant to Section 20 of NGAS Vol. 1 on the next ensuing years and instruct the Municipal Account to facilitate reconciliation of the balances reflecting the balances in the duly certified list of taxpayers.

2.6 We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the amounts of Investment Property and Property, Plant and Equipment Accounts as stated in the Financial Statements; And submit the Report of the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment not later than January 31 of each year.

3.5 We recommended that the Management require the Accounting Office to prepare the Report in Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF on a monthly basis and direct the LDRRMO to submit before the 15th day after the end of each month as required by Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002.

5.5 We recommended that the Management direct the Local Economic Enterprise –In Charge to prepare the 5-year business plan or hire an external consultant if needed as prescribed by Section 4.6 of the Local Budget Circular 111.

5.6 We recommended that the Management instruct the Market Supervisor to prepare and maintain a subsidiary ledger for the market mall consisting of the necessary information of the occupants and the payments of the rental fee. Also, to enforce payment on the delinquent tenants and impose a penalty, if necessary. Moreover, direct the Local Development Council to review the rates of the rental fees on the market mall and to prepare the necessary amendment of its operation, if needed.

6.6 We recommended that the Management strengthen its planning process in identifying and anticipating the activities to be undertaken and the cost to be incurred in holding huge events to ensure that appropriations for the activities are sufficient.

7.5 We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System adhere to the provisions of Section 3.0 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2016-01 and facilitate the conduct of gender analysis using the HGDG tool for gender-responsive PPAs through its GAD Focal Point System.

8.5 We recommended that the Management require the GAD Focal Point to include in the submission of the GAD AR the reports/documents mentioned in Section 4.C.8.5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 to facilitate the monitoring, evaluation, and substantiation of the reported accomplishments.

9.6 We recommended that the Management strictly adhere the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of the DBM and DILG in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

10.8 We recommended that the Management exert extra effort to fully implement its priority programs and projects that are envisioned to help in the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental outcomes; resort to contracting some of its projects to attain economy and efficiency in project implementation; the Municipal Engineer to fast-track the preparation of the program of works; the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to properly plan procurement activities to avoid delayed implementation of projects; the  Local Development Council to judiciously plan the inclusion of priority projects to be included in the 20% Development Fund to avoid revisions and realignment; and the Local Chief Executive to re-create the Project Monitoring Committee/Project Inspection Team that would assist, evaluate and monitor the extent of implementation of programs and projects in order to determine the causes of delay in the implementation.

11.6 We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Sections 37 of Republic Act 9003 in order to deliver to its constituents the maximum benefits of which this Act is created.

12.4 We recommended that the Management direct the BUB Focal Person to monitor the activities to be undertaken to ensure that the project will be completed the soonest possible time since it is already delayed for more than 1 year; and instruct the Municipal Engineer to fast track the completion of the building and ensure that quality service will be delivered to its intended beneficiaries as provided by DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC JMC No. 7.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF to the Auditor on the required date to facilitate the timely audit of the same and that any defect/deficiency noted could be immediately corrected and promptly communicated to management for appropriate action

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the PMR of the Municipality and submit it to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance to Section 12.2 of Revised IRR of RA 9184.
We recommended that Management to initiate the processing of documents for the transfer of ownership of lands owned by the LGU pursuant to the provisions of Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to LBC 111 – Manual on the Setting Up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises and prepare separate set of Financial Statements and separate bank account for each LEE on the next ensuing years.
We recommended that Management prepare the Semi-Annual Financial Self-Sufficiency Review (SFSSR) Report for every Local Economic Enterprises (LEE) starting CY 2018.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Point System to prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report and include in the submission the reports/documents mentioned in Section 4.C.8.5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 to facilitate the monitoring, evaluation and substantiation of the reported accomplishments.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Sections 37 of Republic Act No. 9003 - “An Act Providing for an Ecological Solid Waste Program, Creating the Necessary Institutional Mechanisms and Incentives, Declaring Certain Acts Prohibited and Providing Penalties, Appropriating Funds therefor, and Other Purposes”, to deliver to its constituents the maximum benefits of which this Act is created.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/Property Custodian send demand letters to the concerned accountable officers for the production of the missing government properties and/or report explaining why they were not found during the physical count. Also, advise the accountable officer to request relief from accountability to COA.
We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize   and verify carefully the supporting documents to ensure valid and legal claims are charged to the funds of the LGU.
observations.reason: The supporting documents attached to the disbursement vouchers were valid and complete as observed during post audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the department heads  to completely and properly fill up the purchase orders to ensure that all information pertaining to the procurement at hand are indicated in the purchase order pursuant  to Item B of COA Circular 96-010 and Paragraph II of Sec. 117, LGU-NGAS Manual Volume I.
observations.reason: The Purchase Orders submitted to the Office  had the necessary information.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to purchase their common-used supplies directly from the Procurement Service-DBM and use the PhilGEPS for purchases outside the PS-DBM in compliance to RA 9184, Administrative Order No. 17 and DBM Circular No. 2011-06.
observations.reason: Purchases of common used supplies were made at the PS-DBM  but there were procurements from private suppliers due to the unavailability of the supplies at the DBM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Revenue Collectors to record their collections and deposits/remittances daily and indicate in their cashbooks the daily running balance of unremitted/undeposited collections to facilitate the review and reconciliation of the same.
observations.reason: Verification of the cashbooks of the accountable officers showed that transactions were recorded on a daily basis.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
a.      Limit the payroll deductions to the order of preference provided in the GAA and ensure than an employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the minimum allowed take home pay which is P4,000.00 as provided under Sec. 47 of RA 10924.
b.      Cease from being a withholding agent on loan obligations of employees in institutions that are not among those specified in the provision of the applicable GAA.
observations.reason: Net take home pay of the employees were monitored to ensure it’s within the mandated minimum amount of P4,000.00 for 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Inventory Committee to strictly observe the provisions of Section 124 of the NGAS Manual  Vol. 1  and complete the physical inventory taking by the end of March 2017 and submit to our office the RPCPPE on or before March 31, 2017. We also recommend that management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain property records on all the PPE’s owned by the LGU to safeguard these assets from undetected losses.
observations.reason: Physical inventory of the assets was conducted during the year and the Agency was able to submit the RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the heads of offices to include in their budget proposals all PPAs related to the performance of their mandated functions geared towards the achievement of the goals and objectives of their respective offices to preclude the utilization of appropriations other than those for which these are intended.
observations.reason: Expenses incurred for the DRRMF were provided with an appropriations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the practice of using other funds for expenditures that are not supported with appropriation to avoid the depletion of the affected fund which may result to the delayed implementation of PPAs. We also recommend for the Local Finance Committee and the heads of offices to prepare budget proposals that will answer the needs of their offices to avoid using other funds.
observations.reason: The amount of P42,250.00 was reverted back to the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the MDRRM Officer to prepare a well- planned LDRRMFIP containing all the necessary data and information that will facilitate the evaluation and review of the LDRRMP in relation to the national, regional and provincial planning programs.  We also recommend for the LDRRMC to conduct periodic meetings with the MDRRMO to assess and monitor the implementation of the LDRRMP to ensure that the DRRMF is fully utilized in the implementation of PPAs that will strengthen and improve the capacity of the Municipality to reduce and mitigate disaster risks and to immediately respond to the needs of its populace stricken by calamities.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
1.    Prioritize for funding the acquisition of additional equipment for the collection and disposal of solid waste so that only properly segregated wastes are deposited at the dump site thereby preventing the spilling of garbage to the nearby areas as what happened in the previous year/s.
2.      Follow-up the grant of the BUB for the access road to their new dumpsite for them to expedite transfer and to lessen the disposal of non-segregated waste on their current dumpsite.
3.       Encourage the garbage collectors to segregate, store and sell the plastics, cans and other recyclables to prospective buyers in order to reduce the waste disposed on their open dump site while at the same time, generating income out of the recyclable wastes.
observations.reason: The BUB fund for the access road to the new dumpsite was granted to the LGU on 2017. The project will undergo the usual procurement procedures pursuant to RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management strictly monitor due dates indicated   in the electric bills and to pay these promptly to avoid aversion charges.
observations.reason: The electric bill were paid on time thus no aversion charges were incurred for 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Development Council  prepare the local development plan in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 so that the LGU can maximize the use of the fund to attain its socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes, thus, the constituents will have the opportunity to improve their living and economic condition.
observations.reason: The Agency provided a separate allocation for the GAD programs and was excluded from the 20% DF budget for 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management send its officials and employees to GAD trainings for a better understanding of the GAD Program. We also recommend that management direct its GFPS to conduct consultative meeting with different sectors of the locality to gather information on gender issues and concerns and their causes in order to prepare and formulate GAD Plan and Budget that will address these issues and concerns.
observations.reason: Training and seminars on Gender and development were attended by the GAD Focal Point System.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the GAD Focal Point to include in the submission of the GAD AR the reports/documents mentioned in Section 4.C.8.5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 to facilitate the monitoring, evaluation and substantiation of the reported accomplishments.
observations.managementAction: The Focal person has not prepared the Accomplishment Report thereby the Chief Executive has minimized her travels so that priority will be given to the preparation of GAD accomplishment report.
observations.reason: This audit observation is reiterated on the 2017 report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended requiring the LDRRM Officer to strictly comply with the provision of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 particularly in the preparation of risk reduction and management plan.
observations.managementAction: Instruct the newly designated MDRRMO to strictly comply the provision of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 in the preparation of the LDRRM Plan for CY 2017
observations.reason: The Audit Team has issued a similar finding.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the GAD Focal Point be directed to include in the submission of accomplishment report the report/documents mentioned in Section 5 of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1 to facilitate the review and evaluation of the reported accomplishments.
observations.managementAction: To submit GAD Accomplishments Report with supporting reports and documents. Issued an EO on the reorganization of GAD, FP and the TWG

observations.reason: Performance and warranty securities were paid by the winning bidders.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all Accountable Officers, including the Municipal Treasurer to certify monthly the ending balances in their cashbook in conformity with the provisions of the NGAS.

observations.managementAction: All Accountable Officers are all bonded

observations.reason: Collections were remitted to the liquidating officer on a daily basis.

Fully 
Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended requiring the LDRRM Officer to strictly comply with the provision of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 particularly in the preparation of risk reduction and management plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended directing the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of all paid DVs with original supporting documents to the Auditor’s Office and to follow strictly the regulations on the submission of accounts

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the GAD Focal Point be directed to include in the submission of accomplishment report the report/documents mentioned in Section 5 of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1 to facilitate the review and evaluation of the reported accomplishments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials be required to refund the entire amount paid in compliance to the issued Notice of Disallowance Nos. 16-003-101-(15), 16-004-101-(15) to 16-015-101-(15) without prejudice of the filling of an appeal within six (6) months from the receipt of ND.

observations.recommendation: We recommended requiring the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) and immediately submit the Report of the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Issued EO on Inventory and Disposal Committee. The Committee will conduct the Physical Count and submit to my Office the updated PPE
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should assign personnel among the existing pool of employees of the Municipality under contract of service or plantilla position to assist the existing Inventory Committee to facilitate the regular conduct of physical count of all the properties owned by the Municipality. Thereafter, require the Inventory Committee to prepare a Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant & Equipment (RPCPPE) to be submitted to our Office not later than 31st day of January of each year.
observations.managementAction: Physical Inventory were conducted however, reconciliation is not yet completed.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to observe  Section 59© of Local Treasury Operations Manual particularly such  control measure of:     (a) Stamping serial numbers of the name or logo of the municipality to the cash tickets before issuing to assgined collector;      (b) Practice of affixing initials of collector and date of issuance in the cash tickets before issuance; and    (c) Tearing of cash tickets after collections has been received to avoid possible re-issuance.
observations.managementAction: Only date and initial were indicated at the back of the cash tickets.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer be directed to closely monitor the bank account balances to preclude the issuance of checks without sufficient funds. Likewise, direct the  Municipal Accountant to defer the payment of terminal leave benefit of the former Municipal Treasurer pending settlement of the amount of P30,600.00 which was disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Disallowance has been fully settled.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all accountable officers be bonded pursuant to Section 4.2 of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009. If this is not possible due to funds constraints, assigned only those personnel who are properly bonded to handle cash accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: Not all were bonded. One revenue collector remain unbounded  during conduct of cash examination
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that deputized collector to be directed to strictly turn-over their collection daily as prescribed under Section 29 of NGAS for LGU. Likewise, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly monitor and enforce the daily remittance of collections pursuant to the above-cited provisions.
observations.managementAction: Issued memorandum to all revenue collectors. However, remittance of collections by revenue collectors remained not intact
observations.reason: Partially Implemented
